mission.title: tikv: Support online recovery for Raft majority failure
mission.description: Support online recovery for Raft majority failure. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current tikv deployment
steps.description:
Verify your tiup --version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
helm list -n tikv 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working tikv installation.

steps.title: Review tikv configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant tikv configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl get configmap -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=tikv
```
## Development Task

Currently, we have a tool to recover Raft groups from majority failure:
```
tikv-ctl unsafe-recover remove-fail-stores -s <store_id, store_id> …
tikv-ctl recreate-region -r <region_id> ...

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support online recovery for Raft majority failure
steps.description:
- Update the breakdown of the test, including benchmark the recovery time.
- The prioritization follows the stage. Stage 2 is actually some subtasks of `force_leader`. 
- For the inconsistency fixing tool, let's discuss later. As expected, we won't be able to do them in this sprint.
- For TiFlash
```yaml
tikv-ctl unsafe-recover remove-fail-stores -s <store_id, store_id> …
tikv-ctl recreate-region -r <region_id> ...
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl get events -n tikv --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support online recovery for Raft majority failure" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
- Update the breakdown of the test, including benchmark the recovery time.
- The prioritization follows the stage. Stage 2 is actually some subtasks of `force_leader`. 
- For the inconsistency fixing tool, let's discuss later. As expected, we won't be able to do them in this sprint.
codeSnippets:
tikv-ctl unsafe-recover remove-fail-stores -s <store_id, store_id> …
tikv-ctl recreate-region -r <region_id> ...

prerequisites.description: A running TiKV cluster (standalone or deployed on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator). TiUP and tikv-ctl are the primary CLI tools for managing TiKV.
